image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'The graph of the function f is shown above,' indicating the graph is required
the task asks about limits at specific x-values from a graph, which depends on visual reading of the plotted function
multi_step: the problem is multiple choice with 4 candidate statements, so solving typically requires checking more than one option against the graph
each option refers to a different limit claim (x -> 1, 3, 4, 5), which suggests repeated local inspection/comparison on the graph
the reasoning is not just direct lookup of one value; it involves identifying which statement is false among several graph-based claims
verifiability: choices are provided explicitly, making the target answer set closed and easy to check
answer_text exactly matches one listed choice ('$\\lim_{x \\to 5} f(x) = 4$')
registry_passed=true and no asset issues are listed
the correctness can in principle be verified by inspecting the graph at the specified x-values and comparing with the chosen statement
